rationale: Dahl and Moreau (2002) is not cited as a bare list item; the citing paper summarizes its specific measurement choices (expert originality judgments, consumer willingness-to-pay panels, and small panel sizes) in the prior-work argument. Those details are used to position the citing paper's main contribution: linking raw-idea evaluations to observed market outcomes and decomposing raw-idea-to-design-to-market relationships. The citation is also revisited in the discussion to contrast narrow versus broad ideation scopes when interpreting expert performance, indicating a meaningful framing role.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Dahl and Moreau (2002) as prior work on evaluating raw ideas with expert and consumer judgments, then extends that line by testing whether such early idea assessments predict actual sales outcomes. It also uses Dahl and Moreau's narrower ideation context as a comparison point when interpreting expert-versus-consumer screening performance.
